rationale: The citing paper uses Pocheptsova et al. (2009) repeatedly to build its core mechanism, not as a passing background citation. It specifically draws on that paper's evidence that depletion shifts people toward more intuitive/automatic processing and away from deliberative trade-off processing, and even references its choice-deferral implication. This prior logic is used to justify the paper's hypotheses that weekday (more depleted) shoppers will be more influenced by music-induced affect, which is then tested in the authors' studies.
relation_description: The citing paper treats the cited paper as key conceptual and empirical grounding for the depletion-to-processing-shift mechanism. It then builds its own week part and in-store music framework on top of that mechanism to explain when music affects supermarket sales.
